text:
The happy result of his travels throughout Finland, Dr. Lönnrot now
commenced to arrange under the central idea of a great epic, called
Kalevala, and in February, 1835, the manuscript was transmitted to the
Finnish Literary Society, which had it published in two parts. Lönnrot,
however, did not stop here; he went on searching and collecting, and,
in 1840, had brought together more than one thousand fragments of
epical poetry, national ballads, and proverbs. These he published in
two works, respectively entitled, Kanteletar (Lyre-charm), and The
Proverbs of the Suomi People, the latter containing over 1700 proverbs,
adages, gnomic sentences, and songs.
